recent_thoughts.thought: I'm fascinated by the idea that the Sistema Internacional de Unidades is based on seven fundamental units that can be combined to define any physical magnitude. It's interesting to see how these units have been chosen by convention and have evolved over time, with the most recent revision being approved in 2018. I'm also curious about the fact that the SI is used as a reference for international trade and commerce, ensuring that products meet certain characteristics and are interchangeable. What really catches my attention is the connection between these units and physical phenomena, such as the definition of the second, which I assume is related to the concept of time.

recent_thoughts.thought: I'm fascinated by the concept of systems of writing and how they're tied to oral languages. It's interesting to note that each community has its own language, but the development of writing systems has been slow and irregular. I'm also intrigued by the idea that writing systems can preserve characteristics and expressions that are no longer common in spoken language. The fact that the study of writing systems has developed independently for each script, leading to different terminology, suggests that there's a rich diversity in the ways that languages are written. I'm curious to learn more about the different types of writing systems and how they've evolved over time.

recent_thoughts.thought: I'm fascinated by the concept of breaking down human speech into smaller segments called "fonos" and analyzing their acoustic characteristics. The idea that similar sounds can be identified as the same fono and represented by a single symbol in a transcription system is really interesting. I'm also curious about the process of analyzing speech using spectrographic analysis and how it can help us understand the physical properties of sound. The mention of classes of equivalence and phonemas also caught my attention, as it seems to imply that there's a complex relationship between the physical properties of sound and how they're perceived by speakers of a language.

recent_thoughts.thought: I'm fascinated by the concept of alphabets and how they represent sounds in a language. The fact that alphabets can be adapted and modified to suit the needs of a particular language is really interesting. I'm also intrigued by the mention of logograms and silabarios, which seem to be alternative systems of writing. The history of the alphabet, particularly the origins of the Greek alphabet from the Phoenician alphabet, is also captivating. I'm curious to learn more about how these systems of writing evolved and how they differ from one another. The use of diacritical marks to differentiate sounds and the extension of alphabets with supplementary letters is another aspect that I find interesting.

recent_thoughts.thought: I'm fascinated by the evolution of the Latin alphabet and how it has spread to become the most widely used alphabet in the world. The fact that it originated from the Etruscan alphabet, which in turn was based on the ancient Greek alphabet, is really interesting to me. I'm also curious about the variations of the Latin alphabet that have developed over time, such as the addition of new letters to represent sounds in other languages. The mention of the legendary origin of the Latin alphabet, where Carmenta altered 15 letters of the Greek alphabet to create the Latin alphabet, is also intriguing. I'd like to learn more about the history and development of the Latin alphabet.
